titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Marguerite Young Papers
titlestmt.author: by Andrea Benefiel and Molly Wheeler

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday July 23, 2018 at 20:44

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Marguerite Young Papers document the work of writer Marguerite Young. The papers consist of personal and professional correspondence, drafts of writings, audiovisual material, notebooks, research files, printed material, photographs, artwork, realia, and financial papers spanning the years 1925 to 1999. The bulk of the collection consists of Young's drafts of writings, correspondence, and audiovisual material. Writings include autograph manuscript and typescript drafts, printed versions, notes, and notebooks of her writings, including Angel in the Forest: A Fairy Tale of Two Utopias; Miss Macintosh, My Darling; and Harp Song for a Radical: The Life and Times of Eugene Victor Debs. Correspondence includes letters from friends and colleagues regarding her professional and personal life. Audiovisual material consists of sound recordings and one unidentified film, including the 1974 to 1975 radio production of Miss Macintosh, My Darling read by various authors and actors for WBAI-FM Pacifica Radio. Other papers include photographs, clippings, financial papers, personal papers, personal effects, and realia..

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Marguerite Young on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 1993 and from Daphne Nowling on the Elizabeth Wakeman Dwight Memorial Fund, 2000. Donated by Margaret Hamilton, 1999 and Charles Ruas, 2000.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

Restricted Fragile Materials in boxes 28-36 and 83 (audiovisual material) and box 37 (printed material) may be consulted only with permission of the appropriate curator. Reference copies of most sound recordings are available and are noted when present. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Marguerite Young Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Marguerite Young Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection includes materials previously identified by the following call numbers: Uncat ZA MS 314, Uncat ZA MS 649, Uncat ZA MS 630, and Uncat MSS 52.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, and competing priorities. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

As a rule, descriptive information found in the Collection Contents section is drawn in large part from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing. .
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Printed material received with the collection was removed for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching Orbis (Yale's Online Catalog).

bioghist.head: Marguerite Young (1908-1995)
bioghist.p: Marguerite Young, American author of poetry, fiction, non-fiction, and criticism. Young was born on August 28, 1908 in Indianapolis, Indiana and died on November 17, 1995 in New York City. Her published works include Prismatic Ground (1937), Moderate Fable (1944), Angel in the Forest: A Fairy Tale of Two Utopias (1945), Miss Macintosh, My Darling (1965), and Harp Song for a Radical: The Life and Times of Eugene Victor Debs (1999). Her articles, book reviews, and poetry appeared in magazines and newspapers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Marguerite Young Papers document the work of writer Marguerite Young. The papers consist of personal and professional correspondence, drafts of writings, audiovisual material, notebooks, research files, printed material, photographs, artwork, realia, and financial papers spanning the years 1925 to 1999. The bulk of the collection consists of Young's drafts of writings, correspondence, and audiovisual material. Writings include autograph manuscript and typescript drafts, printed versions, notes, and notebooks of her writings, including Angel in the Forest: A Fairy Tale of Two Utopias ; Miss Macintosh, My Darling ; and Harp Song for a Radical: The Life and Times of Eugene Victor Debs . Correspondence includes letters from friends and colleagues regarding her professional and personal life. Audiovisual material consists of sound recordings and one unidentified film, including the 1974 to 1975 radio production of Miss Macintosh, My Darling read by various authors and actors for WBAI-FM Pacifica Radio. Other papers include photographs, clippings, financial papers, personal papers, personal effects, and realia.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four groupings: I. January 1993 Acquisition, 1925-1995. II. December 1999 Acquisition, 1966-1995. III. January 2000 Acquisition, circa 1940-1994. IV. August 2000 Acquisition, 1999.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: Correspondence, Writings, Sound Recordings, Photographs, and Printed Material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Formerly classified as Uncat Za MS 314.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Marguerite Young on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 1994.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This subseries is arranged alphabetically by correspondent. A small amount of first name only, family, and unidentified correspondence is filed at the end of the subseries.

title.part: Angel in the Forest: A Fairy Tale of Two Utopias

did.unittitle: Unidentified, likely part of Angel in the Forest: A Fairy Tale of Two Utopias
Miss Macintosh, My Darling bound typescript with corrections
Miss Macintosh, My Darling bound typescript with corrections
Various printed versions of writing drafts

Miss Macintosh, My Darling reading for WBAI-FM Pacifica Radio. Readers included Anaïs Nin, Marion Seldes, Alice Playten, H. M. Koutoukas, Leo Lerman, Michael Wagger, Novella Nelson, Osceola Archer, Owen Dodson, Wyatt Cooper, Michael Higgins, Anne Fremantle, Peggy Cass, Ruth Ford, Earle Hymen, and Daisy Alden

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Reference copies available for all readings of Miss Macintosh, My Darling . Photocopies of reel-to-reel boxes with program descriptions are filed at the beginning of Box 24.

did.unittitle: Anaïs Nin introduces and interviews Marguerite Young includes readings by both

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Reference copies available. Photocopies of reel-to-reel boxes with program descriptions are filed at the beginning of Box 24.

did.unittitle: "WBAI-FM Pacifica Radio, Brian Moore reads a chapter of The Lonely Passion of Judith Hearne , followed by an interview with Charles Ruas." 10" reel-to-reel tape [1]
"WNYC, Absence of Silence, Marguerite Young interview with Matthew Paris." 7" reel-to-reel tape [1]
"Marguerite Young's reading, Ruth Stephen Poetry Center, University of Arizona, PMM Auditorium." 7" reel-to-reel tape [1]

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of most sound recordings are available and are noted when present. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Formerly classified as Uncat ZA MS 649.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Daphne Nowling on the Elizabeth Wakeman Dwight Memorial Fund, 2000.

did.unittitle: Documents related to theft of Young's belongings from her apartment

Alfred A. Knopf, Incorporated contract with Young for Harp Song for a Radical: The Life and Times of Eugene Victor Debs
Harp Song for a Radical: The Life and Times of Eugene Victor Debs , photocopies of typescript draft

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Formerly classified as Uncat ZA MS 630.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The bulk of the acquisition contains writing drafts and notebooks. Most writing drafts relate to Harp Song for a Radical: The Life and Times of Eugene Victor Debs . Other papers include correspondence, financial papers, printed materials, and photographs. Realia includes woven fabric, tin boxes, a bracelet, and artwork. Seven audiocassettes filed at the end of the acquisition include readings of Miss Macintosh, My Darling for WBAI-FM Pacifica Radio and a lecture.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of most sound recordings are available and are noted when present. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains bound and galley proofs of Young's Harp Song for a Radical: The Life and Times of Eugene Victor Debs .
